query: Write a SKILL.md for PostgreSQL query optimization in Django. Include N+1 detection, EXPLAIN ANALYZE interpretation, and index selection. It should NOT cover schema migrations.
expected_behavior: Output is a valid SKILL.md file. Frontmatter contains 'name' and 'description' fields. Description follows the [What] [Use for] [Keywords] NOT for [Exclusions] formula and explicitly excludes schema migrations. Body includes at least one anti-pattern with Symptom/Problem/Solution structure and at least one decision table or decision tree. SKILL.md body is under 500 lines. References to any external files (scripts/references/examples) are only made if the skill actually includes those files.

query: My skill description is 'Helps with images' — it's activating on everything and nothing. Rewrite it.
expected_behavior: Output is a rewritten frontmatter description that: (1) names a specific capability rather than generic 'helps with', (2) contains at least 3 trigger keywords or phrases that match real user queries, (3) contains an explicit 'NOT for' clause listing at least 2 adjacent domains the skill should NOT handle, (4) stays within 100–300 characters. Agent also explains WHY the original failed (Description Soup anti-pattern) and names the fix (specific triggers + NOT clauses).

query: My SKILL.md is 920 lines. Split it using progressive disclosure.
expected_behavior: Agent identifies what belongs in SKILL.md (philosophy, decision trees, anti-patterns, 'when to use') vs. references/ (deep dives, exhaustive catalogs, worked examples, edge cases). Agent proposes a concrete file split with target line counts per file, keeping SKILL.md under 500 lines. Agent warns about the Orphaned Sections anti-pattern and requires each new reference file to be triggered from SKILL.md with a conditional 'when X → see Y' statement, not just listed in an index.

query: Convert our 6-step incident-response runbook into a reusable Claude Code skill.
expected_behavior: Agent first asks scope questions (what the runbook covers, what it should NOT cover, target user expertise level) OR makes those assumptions explicit. Output includes: (1) a SKILL.md skeleton with name, description using the required formula, and sections for Philosophy/When to Use/Decision Trees/Anti-Patterns/Scripts, (2) a mapping from the 6 original steps to philosophy-first framing (not a 6-item checklist), (3) at least 2 anti-patterns drawn from common runbook-execution mistakes.

query: Review this SKILL.md and tell me if it will ship.
expected_behavior: Agent audits against at least 4 of the 5 canonical anti-patterns (Reference Illusion, Description Soup, Template Theater, Everything Skill, Orphaned Sections) AND the structural bar (line count < 500, description has triggers + NOT clause, all referenced files exist). Output is ranked by severity (blocker / gap / polish), not a flat list. Verdict is one of: SHIP / IMPROVE / REWORK, with concrete next actions for IMPROVE or REWORK.

query: Should I build this as a skill, a subagent, or an MCP server? I want to wrap the GitHub API so my team can search issues.
expected_behavior: Agent applies the Skill-vs-Subagent-vs-MCP decision tree. Recommends MCP (external API, auth, stateful) with clear reasoning tied to the decision criteria. Explains why Skill is wrong (no runtime state / no API auth) and why Subagent is wrong (API wrapper is not multi-step orchestration). If the agent recommends something other than MCP, it must cite which criterion from the decision tree supports that choice.
